name: Trip planning
goal: End up with booked transport and lodging, a realistic day-by-day spine, and the admin (money, health, documents) handled so the trip runs itself once you leave.
description: Roughly four weeks from “we should go somewhere” to “we’re packed and the boring stuff is sorted.” Tasks assume you might be international, domestic, or driving—skip sections that don’t apply. All calendar dates shift to start from the day you create this plan.

tasks.title: Lock the trip frame (where, when, who, budget)
tasks.content:
Decide the non-negotiables before you spend money.

Decide now:
- Who is traveling (solo / couple / group) and whether anyone has mobility, dietary, or scheduling constraints
- Hard dates vs flexible window; note any blackout dates (work, school, events)
- Total trip budget and a rough split: transport / lodging / food & fun / buffer for surprises
- One sentence: what would make this trip a win?

If international:
- Passport validity (many places want 6 months past return)
- Visa or ETA rules, vaccination or entry forms if they apply

If driving:
- Max hours per day you’re willing to drive; overnight stops if it’s multi-day

Done when:
- You can name destination(s) and exact or bounded dates
- You have a written budget range and a shared notes doc or thread everyone agrees to use

tasks.title: Book how you get there and back
tasks.content:
Price usually rises as inventory shrinks—book the longest-lead option first (often flights, sometimes ferries or trains).

Before you pay:
- Compare 2–3 real itineraries, not just the cheapest (layover pain, bag fees, refund rules)
- Read change and cancellation terms; note them in your trip doc
- If renting a car: check age fees, one-way drop charges, and whether you need an IDP

After you book:
- One place for confirmations (folder, trip app, or pinned email)
- Calendar holds for departures, arrivals, and check-in windows (airline apps often miss the mental load)
- Set a reminder to check in / select seats if your carrier opens that early

Done when:
- Return journey is covered, not just outbound
- Someone else could find every confirmation in under two minutes

tasks.title: Reserve lodging that matches how you’ll actually move
tasks.content:
Pick base location from how you’ll spend time: walking, transit, or parking—not just from pretty photos.

Shortlist 3–5 options, then filter:
- Recent reviews mentioning noise, stairs, AC/heat, Wi‑Fi, and honest photos
- Cancellation policy vs how sure your dates are
- Check-in/out times and whether you can leave bags on arrival or departure day
- Safety: read the neighborhood at the times you’ll be out (evening if you like late dinners)

Money abroad:
- Note whether places expect cash; plan one ATM or card that works without huge fees
- Inform your bank you’re traveling if they still want travel notices

Done when:
- Every night is covered (or consciously left open for a flexible leg)
- You know how you get from each arrival hub (airport/station) to the door

tasks.title: Shape the days, book the bottlenecks, prep the trip kit
tasks.content:
You want a spine, not a spreadsheet—must-dos, a few timed bookings, and blank space.

Itinerary:
- List 3–5 “if we do nothing else” items; cluster them by neighborhood to cut transit zigzags
- Reserve what sells out: popular museums, guides, special restaurants, national-park entries
- Build in one low-key day if the trip is longer than a long weekend

Share & sync:
- Send travel partners a one-page outline + lodging and transport links
- Agree how you’ll split costs (one card, split app, or cash kitty)

Offline & safety:
- Offline maps for key areas; screenshot or write down lodging address in local script if useful
- Photos of passports/IDs in a secure cloud note; emergency contacts
- Travel insurance decision (health + trip cancellation) if the stakes warrant it

Done when:
- Bottlenecks are booked or consciously skipped
- Packing list started; chargers/adapters/meds on it
- Everyone knows the first 48 hours hour-by-hour
